headline: Singapore charged three over Nvidia chips routed via Malaysia. Johor's boom runs on the same chips.
context: On 27 Feb 2025, Singapore charged three men with fraud tied to servers carrying Nvidia AI chips that moved Singapore to Malaysia. Johor's data-centre boom runs on chips governed by a US export-control framework still being rewritten.

cards.big: Singapore charged three men over Nvidia AI server sales on 27 Feb 2025: a Singaporean, a permanent resident, a Chinese national. The route ran Singapore to Malaysia.
cards.sub: US prosecutors are separately probing whether chips that landed in Malaysia reached a Chinese AI firm. Johor's data-centre boom runs on the same supply chain.

cards.big: The 'Tier 2' label came from a rule that never took effect. Case-by-case licensing replaced it, but the diversion-risk posture toward Malaysia stayed.
cards.sub: BIS issued the Diffusion Rule on 13 Jan 2025. Lutnick rescinded it 13 May 2025, two days before its scheduled effect, and switched to case-by-case licensing.

cards.big: Johor has 80+ announced data-centre projects and over 5 GW in announced capacity, about a quarter of Peninsular peak demand if all are built.
cards.sub: YTL operates Sedenak Tech Park; ByteDance and GDS have Johor footprints. The thesis assumes US-controlled AI chips keep flowing.

cards.big: Fourteen months after MITI promised to investigate any Malaysian company named in a US probe, no Malaysian company has been publicly named.
cards.sub: Tengku Zafrul (then MITI) made the commitment in March 2025. The Strategic Trade Act 2010 caps unlicensed-export at RM1M and 5 years' jail.

cards.big: Johor's data-centre boom and Singapore's chip case tell one story. Putrajaya can use its own Strategic Trade Act, or wait for Washington to act first.
Like building the largest port on a known smuggling route, then asking the customs office across the border to keep clearing it.
cards.sub: Johor's data-centre build-out runs on US-controlled chips. Three men were charged across the strait for moving them.

cards.big: Singapore charged three men. The chips moved through Malaysia. Fourteen months on, no Malaysian case has been announced. Washington still has the move.
